items.title: Man City sweep past Brighton in FA Cup final to complete domestic double
items.summary: Manchester City completed the domestic double after beating Brighton 4-0 in the Women's FA Cup final at Wembley.

items.title: Stick or twist for Arteta - keep to the formula or refine Arsenal style?
items.summary: Arsenal are fresh from their Champions League final heartbreak but thoughts are already moving to what they need to do to improve next season.

items.title: Wayne Rooney BODIES Chelsea legend Michael Essien and sends him flying to the floor in Soccer Aid 2026
items.summary: WAYNE ROONEY was straight back into his old ways in the opening minutes of Soccer Aid as he put in a crunching tackle on an old rival. Rooney, 40, started as a second striker for England at the iconic charity football match. The annual match, held this year at West Ham's London Stadium, raises money

items.title: Liverpool to open formal talks with Iraola
items.summary: Liverpool will open formal talks with Andoni Iraola this week over becoming their new head coach.

items.title: Ice, Ice Baby and 'start of a new era' - best of Arsenal parade
items.summary: For the first time in 22 years, Arsenal are the Premier League champions - and the players, staff and fans got to celebrate on Sunday with an incredible bus parade through the streets of north London.

items.title: Konate expresses sadness over Liverpool exit
items.summary: France defender Ibrahima Konate says he will leave Liverpool with "lifelong memories" after experiencing "highs and lows" during his time at Anfield.

items.title: ‘This is a new one’ – Fans can’t believe where Soccer Aid 2026 is being played
items.summary: FANS poked fun at West Ham's relegation as they watched Soccer Aid take place at London Stadium. The Hammers were relegated from the Premier League last Sunday following Tottenham's 1-0 win over Everton. Seven days on, London Stadium played host to Soccer Aid. items.title: Sky forced to apologise after Arsenal fan hijacks bus parade coverage and swears on live TV
items.summary: SKY NEWS were forced to apologise when a jubilant Arsenal fan made a foul-mouthed comment live on air. The Gunners' biggest names had been on a trophy parade through North London. And, having ended a 22-year wait to be crowned the Premier League champions, hundreds of thousands of Arsenal supporters

items.title: PSG celebrate Champions League title victory in Paris
items.summary: PSG supporters and players celebrate Paris Saint-Germain's second Champions League title victory.

items.title: Wales call up teenage Leeds left-back Lienou
items.summary: Teenage Leeds United left-back Jayden Lienou is called up to the senior Wales squad for the first time for friendly matches against Ghana and Romania.

items.title: Hundreds arrested and police injured after Champions League riots in France
items.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

items.title: South Africa made to look like fools over World Cup visa issues, says minister
items.summary: After a day of uncertainty, the government confirms the team will depart for Mexico on Monday.
